2603.20980.title: From Causal Discovery to Dynamic Causal Inference in Neural Time Series
2603.20980.paper_summary: Time-varying causal models provide a powerful framework for studying dynamic scientific systems, yet most existing approaches assume that the underlying causal network is known a priori - an assumption rarely satisfied in real-world domains where causal structure is uncertain, evolving, or only indirectly observable. This limits the applicability of dynamic causal inference in many scientific settings. We propose Dynamic Causal Network Autoregression (DCNAR), a two-stage neural causal modeling framework that integrates data-driven causal discovery with time-varying causal inference. In the first stage, a neural autoregressive causal discovery model learns a sparse directed causal network from multivariate time series. In the second stage, this learned structure is used as a structural prior for a time-varying neural network autoregression, enabling dynamic estimation of causal influence without requiring pre-specified network structure. We evaluate the scientific validity of DCNAR using behavioral diagnostics that assess causal necessity, temporal stability, and sensitivity to structural change, rather than predictive accuracy alone. Experiments on multi-country panel time-series data demonstrate that learned causal networks yield more stable and behaviorally meaningful dynamic causal inferences than coefficient-based or structure-free alternatives, even when forecasting performance is comparable. These results position DCNAR as a general framework for using AI as a scientific instrument for dynamic causal reasoning under structural uncertainty.
2603.20980.paper_summary_zh: 時間變化的因果模型提供了一個強大的框架，用於研究動態科學系統，然而大多數現有的方法假設潛在的因果網絡是事先已知的——這在因果結構不確定、發展中或僅能間接觀察的現實世界領域中很少滿足。這限制了動態因果推斷在許多科學環境中的適用性。我們提出了動態因果網絡自迴歸（DCNAR），這是一個兩階段的神經因果建模框架，將數據驅動的因果發現與時間變化的因果推斷相結合。在第一階段，一個神經自迴歸因果發現模型從多變量時間序列中學習一個稀疏的有向因果網絡。在第二階段，這個學到的結構被用作時間變化神經網絡自迴歸的結構先驗，從而實現因果影響的動態估計，而無需預先指定網絡結構。我們使用行為診斷來評估DCNAR的科學有效性，這些診斷評估因果必要性、時間穩定性和對結構變化的敏感性，而不僅僅是預測準確性。對多國面板時間序列數據的實驗表明，學習到的因果網絡比基於係數或無結構的替代方案產生更穩定且行為上有意義的動態因果推斷，即使預測性能相當。這些結果使DCNAR成為在結構不確定性下，將AI作為動態因果推理的科學工具的通用框架。
2603.20980.author: Valentina Kuskova et.al.
2603.20980.authors: Valentina Kuskova, Dmitry Zaytsev, Michael Coppedge
